danger_signals.visual_form: incoming bullet/missile patterns from top of screen
large enemy unit entering arena, distinct from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: escalated danger, major combat encounter ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: visual clustering and screen saturation with hostile units

safety_signals.visual_form: dropped item from defeated enemies, distinct visual signature
safety_signals.meaning: temporary invincibility available, window of safety

safety_signals.visual_form: absence of enemy units in immediate vicinity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: helicopter vs. armored jeep as selectable player vehicles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player weapon effects, forward-firing gun upgrades

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: standardized hostile units with distinct boss variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: SWIV missile carriers (named in manual lore), varying enemy silhouettes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: constant enemy spawn from top of scrolling level, projectile streams
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player vehicle moves defensively, enemies advance downscreen

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: large distinct enemy unit replacing standard enemy waves
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: boss-specific attack patterns, strategic positioning required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: shield pickup item visible on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player actively pursues powerup, temporary invincibility granted upon contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scrolling environment changes indicating level advancement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemy difficulty and variety escalates across levels

environmental_storytelling.example: helicopter and armored jeep represent dual-specialization approach; game design reflects Silkworm heritage of mixed unit types
boss enemies visually distinct and scaled larger than standard units, communicating threat escalation

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: boss destruction yields visible gun upgrades to player's forward-firing weapon

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: descends from top, fires projectiles on predictable intervals
occupies significant screen space, executes complex attack patterns distinct from standard enemies
can collide with enemies without taking damage after shield pickup
forward-firing gun displays increased visual intensity or rate after boss defeat

notes: Firepower 2000 (SNES port of SWIV) relies on core shmup visual language: scrolling threat, projectile avoidance, power escalation through pickups. Limited faction complexity — binary player vs. enemy structure. Visual storytelling embedded in vehicle selection mechanic (helicopter vs. jeep duality) and boss design hierarchy. The SWIV acronym in-world refers to a missile type, adding minimal lore layer. Manifestation primarily mechanical rather than narrative-driven.
